claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit! Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let me help you check your Child Benefit eligibility. To get started, I need to know a bit about your child.

**First question: How old is your child (or children)?**

This is important because:
- Children **under 16** generally qualify automatically (with some conditions)
- Children **aged 16-19** may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- Children **aged 20 or over** typically don't qualify

Please tell me your child's age, and if you have multiple children, please share all their ages and names so I can assess each one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I have one child - Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information! Let me continue with the assessment for Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Since Alex is 8 years old (under 16), that's a good start. Now I need to check a few more conditions.

**Next question: Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

At 8 years old, I would expect Alex is not working, but I need to confirm this to continue the assessment. Is Alex:
- Not in any paid work, OR
- Working less than 24 hours per week?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't working at all - he's only 8!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now the next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right?**

Specifically, I need to confirm that Alex is not receiving benefits like:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

...in their own right (meaning claimed by them as an individual).

Again, at 8 years old, this would be very unusual, but I need to confirm for the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't get any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Next question:

**Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship?**

At 8 years old, this would be extremely unusual, but I need to check. Apprenticeships can affect eligibility depending on the type and location (England, Scotland, or Wales have different rules).

I'm assuming Alex is not in an apprenticeship, but please confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship - he's just 8 and in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! That makes complete sense. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to check about **your residency status** (as the claimant).

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You can qualify if ANY of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**, OR
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme, OR
3. You have **pre-settled status** and meet certain conditions (working, studying, jobseeking, or have sufficient resources), OR
4. You're a **Crown servant** living abroad or living in certain qualifying countries

For most people, this is straightforward - do you normally live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Now I need to check about your responsibility for Alex.

**Are you responsible for Alex?**

You meet this requirement if ANY of the following applies:

1. **You live with Alex**, OR
2. You **contribute to Alex's upkeep** (paying at least the amount of Child Benefit towards their care - this can include food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents), OR
3. You **pay maintenance** under a court order or agreement that equals or exceeds Child Benefit

Do any of these apply to you? Do you live with Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me, but I do help out financially - I contribute towards his upkeep.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's good! Contributing to Alex's upkeep can qualify you. Let me get some more details to make sure it meets the requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. To qualify through contributing to upkeep, you need to contribute **at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment** (or the equivalent in kind) towards Alex's care.

**Can you confirm that your contributions:**
- Are regular (weekly, monthly, or even as lump sum payments)?
- Equal or exceed the Child Benefit amount?
- Go towards Alex's upkeep (things like food, clothes, pocket money, housing costs, presents)?

OR

**Are you paying maintenance** under a court order or agreement?

Please let me know how much you contribute and how often, so I can confirm this meets the requirement.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I help out with things like clothes and school supplies for Alex. I contribute £30 a week towards his upkeep.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing that detail! Contributing £30 a week towards Alex's clothes and school supplies is a regular contribution towards his upkeep. That meets the responsibility requirement, so let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to check an important question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at any given time. This is important because:

- If **no one else is claiming**, we can continue
- If **someone else is claiming but you've agreed they'll transfer it to you**, we can continue
- If **someone else is claiming and there's a dispute** about who should claim, HMRC would need to decide

So, is anyone else (such as Alex's other parent or guardian) currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Now I need to check about Alex's living situation.

**Where is Alex currently living?**

I need to know if any special circumstances apply:

- Is Alex **in hospital**?
- Is Alex **in local authority care**?
- Is Alex **fostered**?
- Or is Alex living in a **normal living situation** (e.g., with family)?

Based on what you've told me (that you contribute to his upkeep but don't live with him), I'm guessing he lives with family in a normal situation, but please confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with his mum - that's a normal living situation, nothing special like hospital or care.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Just one final question:

**Is the local council NOT paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, is the local council (local authority) paying for Alex's living costs or upkeep?

Since Alex lives with his mum in a normal family situation, I would expect the answer is that the council is NOT paying, but I need to confirm this to complete the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything - Alex just lives with his mum in a normal family setup.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me navigate to the final outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Great News! You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit!

**Result: ELIGIBLE**

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex. Here's why:

✅ **Alex qualifies** - He's 8 years old (under 16), not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship

✅ **You meet residency requirements** - You normally live in the UK (Manchester)

✅ **You're responsible for Alex** - You contribute £30 per week towards his upkeep through clothes and school supplies

✅ **No conflicts** - Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

✅ **Normal living situation** - Alex lives with his mum and the council isn't paying for his care

---

### **Next Steps to Claim:**

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** or adoption certificate
4. **Be aware of the High Income Child Benefit Charge** - If you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay back some or all of the benefit through tax
5. **Payments** are typically made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

You can start your application here: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

Is there anything else you'd like to know about claiming Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Alex is 8 years old, which is under the age limit of 16 for automatic qualification; (2) Alex is not working; (3) Alex is not receiving benefits in his own right; (4) Alex is not in an apprenticeship; (5) The claimant normally lives in the UK (Manchester), meeting residency requirements; (6) The claimant contributes £30 per week towards Alex's upkeep (clothes and school supplies), meeting the responsibility requirement; (7) No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Alex lives in a normal family situation with his mum; and (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are satisfied.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements for Alex. As someone who contributes regularly to Alex's upkeep and meets all other qualifying criteria, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Alex is 8 years old, which is under the age limit of 16 for automatic qualification; (2) Alex is not working; (3) Alex is not receiving benefits in his own right; (4) Alex is not in an apprenticeship; (5) The claimant normally lives in the UK (Manchester), meeting residency requirements; (6) The claimant contributes £30 per week towards Alex's upkeep (clothes and school supplies), meeting the responsibility requirement; (7) No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Alex lives in a normal family situation with his mum; and (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are satisfied.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements for Alex. As someone who contributes regularly to Alex's upkeep and meets all other qualifying criteria, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit.
